across.clue: a short or temporary period when you fail or forget to do things in the right way.
a limited amount of something especially food that you are allowed to have for example when there is not much available or when someone else is controlling it.
to officially order someone to come to a place especially a court of law
to make someone or something seem less valuable or respected
to measure or describe something as a quantity.
To think very carefully about something for a long time.
to be the same as something else or very much like it
a slow and gradual change from one situation or opinion to another.
Maintain your beliefs | Stand by/stick to your principles
an action or movement that you need care or skill to do.
A burden or source of guilt that constantly weighs on you and is difficult or impossible to escape | an ___ around your neck

down.clue: To officially stop something from being done especially by making it illegal
To give someone the freedom to do what they want for example by taking them out of a situation in which their behaviour is controlled
Back up your words with action | Put your ___ where your mouth is
Very small or very young — used humorously to describe someone, especially a child, at an earlier time in their life | knee-high to a ___
Prepare yourself mentally or physically | ___ yourself
the happiness you feel when you have everything you want and you enjoy your life.
To apply or direct force, influence, skill, or pressure on something in order to achieve a result | ___ to bear
